title: Neoarytera collina
taxonomy: Neoarytera collina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the genus Neoarytera. This plant is native to Australia, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. The Sapindaceae family includes other plants commonly known as soapberry or soap pod trees. As a member of this family, Neoarytera collina shares certain characteristics with its relatives.
uses: Neoarytera collina is reportedly used in traditional medicine in Australia. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are used to treat various ailments. However, further research is needed to confirm the full scope of its medicinal uses.

This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain, fighting infections, and protecting against cell damage.
